description_long: This helmet features a robust, angular design with a predominantly green exterior accented by gray panels on the top and rear. The surface is segmented with geometric ridges and recessed areas, giving it a futuristic, armored appearance. The front section is characterized by a central circular vent and two raised, angular protrusions, while the rear displays additional molded details and subtle panel lines. The helmet appears to be constructed from a hard, impact-resistant plastic or composite material, with no visible visor or external attachments. There are no visible brand logos, graphics, or text, and the overall finish is matte with some areas showing slight surface wear. The object is sized to fit a human head and is likely intended for personal protection in urban environments.
description: A green and gray, angular helmet with a matte finish, featuring geometric ridges and a central front vent, designed for head protection.
description_view_0: Front view shows the central circular vent, two angular protrusions, and the main gray panel on top.
description_view_1: Left-rear view highlights the molded panel lines, subtle surface wear, and additional geometric detailing.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the gray top panel, rear ridges, and a lack of any visible vent or logo.
description_view_3: Right-rear view mirrors the left, with similar panel lines, geometric shapes, and surface wear.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: One circular vent at the front center, no other visible vents.
